headline: Americans are saving just 2.6% of their income — a 65-year low
context: Down from 5.8% a year ago. Outside the 2022 revenge-spending era, the savings rate has almost never been this low in modern history.

headline: The Fed's dot plot just flipped — median rate target now 3.8%
context: In Chair Warsh's first meeting, the median policymaker projects rates ending 2026 higher than today. A rate hike is back on the table.

headline: Three tickers making moves this week

items.reason: Revenue beat by $6B ($41.5B vs $35B expected) — stock up 324% YTD on the AI memory supercycle
Custom AI chips gaining on Nvidia — $1.73T market cap, 25% upside to fair value estimates
Fear & Greed at 17 (Extreme Fear) near $60K — RSI approaching oversold at 33

headline: 59% of Americans can't cover a $1,000 emergency
context: Meanwhile, 37% of households rely on credit for everyday expenses. Wage growth at 3.6% is now lagging 3.8% inflation.

headline: Investing $27.40 a day could grow to $4.4 million
context: Assuming a 10% annual return over 40 years. 88% of millionaires built their wealth through consistent habits — not inheritance.
